step_by_step_reasoning: First, AWS Control Tower onboarding creates a separate tenant account and provisions Amazon QuickSight. Second, the EKS-based multi-tenant SaaS application processes and stores loan data into dedicated tenant schemas in Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL. Third, bank analysts access tenant-isolated QuickSight dashboards, which retrieve database data via AWS Transit Gateway and blend it with on-premises data.

graph.name: Summit Technology Group Single Tenant Data Consumption Architecture
graph.notes: A multi-tenant SaaS loan origination system leveraging account isolation via Control Tower and single-tenant analytics dashboards using Amazon QuickSight.

nodes.name: AWS Control Tower
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Automated onboarding with Account Factory for Terraform (AFT).

nodes.name: SaaS Platform on EKS
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Hosts isolated tenant pods processing loan originations.

nodes.name: Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Multi-tenant database utilizing separate schemas per tenant.

nodes.name: AWS Transit Gateway
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Interconnects tenant VPCs with central database VPC.

nodes.name: Amazon QuickSight
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Tenant-isolated BI dashboards in single-tenant accounts.

nodes.name: On-Premises Database
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Customer on-premises legacy systems connected via VPN.

edges.notes: SaaS app processes loans and stores data in tenant schema.
Triggers automated tenant account creation and QuickSight provisioning.
Routes query traffic to the central multi-tenant VPC.
Delivers query results back to QuickSight.
Pulls bank's on-prem database records into dashboard.
